summary: This is a very brief teaser post that serves as a promotional link to Joel Spolsky's longer essay 'How Microsoft Lost the API War.' The post consists of a single provocative quote from the essay describing two opposing forces within Microsoft, framed as 'The Raymond Chen Camp' and 'The MSDN Magazine Camp.' It functions as a hook to drive readers to the full article rather than presenting a standalone argument.
key_points: The post is a teaser linking to the full essay 'How Microsoft Lost the API War'
It highlights internal tension at Microsoft between two philosophical camps
Raymond Chen represents backward compatibility and developer trust, while MSDN Magazine represents the push toward new, breaking technologies
money_quotes: There are two opposing forces inside Microsoft, which I will refer to, somewhat tongue-in-cheek, as The Raymond Chen Camp and The MSDN Magazine Camp.
key_insight: Microsoft's internal tension between backward compatibility advocates and new-technology evangelists shaped its strategic trajectory in the API wars.
